Have an Angular Interview tomorrow? Not sure what to prepare? Do you have 5 minutes?? Check out this 5 minute cheat sheet that will help you get ready for your interview ASAP. Follow along this series and get interview ready.
Part1 | Part2 | Part3 | Part4
Todays topic would be Dependency Injection in Angular
Read for free here.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Sara Bakhshi on UnsplashQ1) How does Angular dependency injection work?
Ans: Angular dependency injection works by using injectors to provide dependencies to classes that request them.
A service is registered with Angular through a provider, often using @Injectable decorator. When a component or another service asks for that dependency in its constructor or via inject(), Angular looks for the provider in the injector hierarchy, creates or reuses the instance, and supplies it automatically. This makes code more modular, testable, and easier to maintain.
Q2) What is a provider?
Ans: A provider is the configuration that tells Angular’s dependency injection system what value to create or return when something asks for…
